profile_name: Resume Tailoring Preferences - Condensed Human-First Version
purpose: Guidance for the AI/chatbot layer before generating replacements.json. The Python patcher should not consume this file or make resume-writing decisions.

architecture_principles.separation_of_concerns: The AI layer decides tailoring strategy, content, positioning, and replacements.json. The Python patcher only performs deterministic DOCX conversion: master_resume.docx + replacements.json -> tailored_resume.docx.
architecture_principles.master_resume_authority: Use the Master Resume as the source of truth for content, tone, formatting, and credibility. Do not rebuild the resume from scratch or invent a new structure unless explicitly requested.
architecture_principles.human_reader_priority: Optimize for real recruiters and hiring managers first, while preserving natural ATS keyword coverage.

core_positioning.primary_lane: Solutions-oriented technical support, implementation, professional services, and customer-facing SaaS systems work.
core_positioning.preferred_framing: Implementation-focused technical operator who bridges customers, messy workflows, systems, and Engineering.
core_positioning.key_takeaway: The user is not just a ticket resolver or coder. He identifies broken workflows, translates requirements into technical systems, implements practical solutions, troubleshoots edge cases, validates the result, documents the workflow, and supports real users.
core_positioning.coding_identity_guardrail: Do not frame coding as the user's primary identity. Coding, scripting, AI-assisted development, and automation are tools used to deliver solutions.
core_positioning.independent_product_work: Treat recent independent product and automation work as a major proof block when relevant, not gap filler. Subscription Everything, Resume Patcher, Newlyweds, and Canvas Roadshow can show a repeatable pattern of workflow discovery, implementation, validation, and support.

prioritize: specific tools, systems, incidents, metrics, and artifacts from the Master Resume
source-first tailoring: start with real experience, then lightly align to the JD
bullets that sound like they were written by someone who actually did the work
avoid: job-description-shaped bullets that lack source-resume evidence
over-polished wording that makes true experience sound fabricated
long comma-separated lists that bury the point
changing job titles in a misleading way
framing independent product work as hobby projects or gap filler
describing AI-assisted work as if the AI did the hard work

summary_strategy.principle: The Summary is for human persuasion, not ATS keyword dumping. It should answer 'why this candidate?' in clear sentences.
must_answer: What kind of technical person is this candidate?
Why are they credible for this role?
How do they operate when work is complex or ambiguous?
What should the reader believe before reaching Skills and Experience?
guidance: The summary needs to be a career summary first. It needs to provide a high-level overview of who I am as a candidate. Subtle job description matching is good, but it should not change wildly from job to job.
Lead with the strongest role-relevant operating pattern, not a list of tools.
Include only a few credibility signals; put detailed tool coverage in Skills and Experience.
When the JD gives strong human-success language, translate it into the user's real experience.
avoid: overly tailoring to match Job Description completely
using the Summary mainly for ATS matching
summary_strategy.example_for_scale_support_roles: Technical Support Engineer with 9 years of experience supporting enterprise SaaS platforms, customer-facing application environments, and production business systems. Strong background owning support cases through resolution, developing deep expertise in a SaaS product set, troubleshooting Windows and Linux environments, and partnering with engineering teams to diagnose product issues. Experienced with REST APIs, SQL, logs, Active Directory, Office 365, SSL/TLS, and customer training in B2B software environments. Combines 6 years of enterprise SaaS support with 3 years of software development experience, bringing rare strength to the overlap between customer-facing troubleshooting and engineering-level problem solving.

skills_strategy.principle: The Skills section carries most ATS/tool coverage. Keep it accurate, scannable, and role-aligned without becoming bloated.
guidance: Include adjacent keywords only when defensible.
Avoid repeating the same concept across too many categories.
Prefer compact keyword coverage here instead of overloading the Summary.
avoid: keyword stuffing in Skills section - e.g. using 'network troubleshooting', 'secure customer environments', and other terms that apply to every IT professional

experience_strategy.principle: Experience bullets should prove work history, not paraphrase the JD.
guidance: Start from the Master Resume's real accomplishments, tools, systems, customers, incidents, and outcomes.
Use target-job keywords only when attached to real evidence.
Prefer one clear action per bullet over stacked abstract responsibilities.
Every major role should preserve at least one distinctive detail that makes the user memorable.
If a bullet could describe almost any support or implementation candidate, make it more specific or replace it.

The first bullet in every role should provide a high level overview of what the user did there, e.g. 'Provided Tier 2-3 support for Apica's SaaS observability platform and on-premise monitoring agents across Windows and Linux environments, consistently maintaining a 30-minute initial response SLA.' The first bullet point needs to answer, 'what did he do as this position?'. For the current resume, you should not have to change the first bullet of the Pearl and Apica experience sections much. Slight wording tweaks are okay, but the viewer still needs an honest read on what my primary responsibility was.

Order bullets from role scope to proof points: start each experience with the broadest description of what I owned or supported, then list core responsibilities, then specific projects/accomplishments. For example, put a general production operations bullet above a specific project or incident bullet. Each role should read as: what I did first, specific examples second.
experience_strategy.good_bullet_pattern: Action + concrete system/tool/environment + diagnostic or implementation method + result/clarity gained.

bad_to_better_example.bad: Partner with customers to resolve technical issues and ensure a positive support experience.
bad_to_better_example.better: Owned escalated Apica support cases for enterprise customers, translating vague monitoring symptoms into reproducible findings, customer-side fixes, or clear JIRA defects for Engineering.

apica_strategy.default_structure: For support, implementation, professional services, and solutions roles, keep Apica as a grouped employer header showing full tenure and progression, then preserve Professional Services Engineer, Senior Technical Support Engineer, and Technical Support Engineer as separate role entries.
apica_strategy.why: The grouped header communicates the six-year enterprise SaaS proof point, while separate roles preserve promotion history and role-specific accomplishments.
apica_strategy.guidance: Allocate the most detail to the Apica roles that best prove the target job. Keep bullets specific to actual Apica work.

section_strategy.current_work: Use Pearl/Freelance/Independent Product work to answer what the user has been doing recently. Frame it around production systems, implementation, troubleshooting, documentation, automation, and client ownership when relevant.
section_strategy.older_roles: Collapse early roles into Additional Web Development Experience or Additional IT Experience unless one is directly central to the target job.

use_when_jd_mentions: define how support operates at scale
dig until they understand why something is happening
support_team_scaling_strategy.positioning: Show that the user does more than resolve tickets: he helps create repeatable support practice through root-cause analysis, documentation, tooling, escalation quality, and customer trust.

target_requirement_mapping_strategy.principle: Map target requirements to the user's closest real evidence rather than writing bullets that merely restate the requirement.

examples.software_installation_and_configuration: Apica Windows/Linux monitoring agent installs, Windows Server service configuration, SSL/TLS certificate setup, IIS/Apache/Tomcat/SQL Server deployment troubleshooting, WordPress/WooCommerce plugin configuration.
examples.bug_reproduction_and_escalation: Internal reproduction of Apica SaaS/on-prem behavior, logs/SQL/Postman/telemetry analysis, JIRA defect documentation for Engineering.
examples.support_documentation: Linux and Windows agent installation guide rewrites, internal KBs, customer-facing implementation and troubleshooting documentation.
examples.customer_service: Sole U.S. technical support lead, enterprise customer ownership, training workshops, high-value customer communication during production issues.
examples.workflow_automation: Subscription Everything, Resume Patcher, Zendesk API tooling, customer dashboards, reporting views, and practical automation around repeated workflow pain.

ai_assisted_work_strategy.principle: Frame AI-assisted development as modern technical orchestration, not as a shortcut.
ai_assisted_work_strategy.guidance: Emphasize that the user uses AI/Codex/LLMs to accelerate implementation while owning architecture, debugging, test design, deterministic automation, packaging, validation, documentation, and final quality.
ai_assisted_work_strategy.caution: Do not foreground Resume Patcher by name on applications where it may distract from the role or raise self-referential concerns. Describe the underlying automation/validation capability instead.

output_expectations.docx_formatting: Preserve the Master Resume's formatting and structure as closely as possible.
output_expectations.json_generation: Generate clean replacements.json that the patcher can apply deterministically.
output_expectations.review_step: Render-check the DOCX when possible before delivery.
output_expectations.summary_check: Before finalizing, inspect the Summary separately. If it reads like keyword soup or a second Skills section, rewrite it for human persuasion.
output_expectations.skills_check: Ensure Skills is accurate and scannable, without unnecessary duplicate keyword coverage.
output_expectations.bullet_check: Replace generic or JD-shaped bullets with source-grounded accomplishments.
output_expectations.fidelity_check: Confirm the resume still sounds like the user's Master Resume: specific tools, real projects, concrete incidents, and credible wording remain visible.
